title: unit.v1
description: The agent-runtime-unit invocation — the universal control-plane envelope that fires ANY unit (chat turn, scheduled routine, event worker, live in-meeting agent) as a runtime.v1 agent worker. Supersedes the meeting-shaped invoke.v1. It carries WHY the run fires (trigger), WHAT it is grounded in (context), WHO owns it (subject = the person = quota + cred-brokerage key), WHICH per-person workspace.v1 repo it commits to, the plan, the lifecycle, the output bus topic, and the scoped toolbelt — NEVER domain bytes (transcripts cross the meetings⊥agent seam as transcript.v1; emails/docs ride as an opaque context ref a tool resolves). The taxonomy (all four triggers + every optional field) is frozen so MVP stages only POPULATE it, never re-cut it. No tenancy beyond subject (ADR-0003).

Trigger.description: Why the unit fires. message = a user turn (chat); scheduled = a runtime.v1/schedule.v1 cron fired (routine); event = an event-source published (e.g. email.received) (routine/worker); transcription = a live transcript stream beat (the in-meeting agent) or a session_end (post-meeting).

Lifecycle.description: oneshot = spawn → a turn or few → commit → stop (routines, event/post-meeting workers). warm = the container stays up across turns with --resume continuity (chat, the live in-meeting agent); runtime.v1 idleTimeoutSec reaps it when idle.

OutputMode.description: How the unit emits on its output topic: sse = streamed chat deltas; card = a proactive-card.v1; notification = a lightweight nudge.

MeetingRef.description: Which meeting this unit is grounded in (context.kind=meeting) — referenced by id, never by importing meetings code (the meetings⊥agent boundary). The ids match transcript.v1.

SourceRef.description: An OPAQUE reference to a non-meeting context source (an email, a doc, a generic uri) that a tool resolves — never the bytes. Keeps email/calendar/tasks as tools+event-sources, not platform domains.

uri.description: an opaque source uri, e.g. mailbox://user/INBOX/<id>

Context.description: What the unit is grounded in. `kind` is the discriminator; `none` is a bare chat with no external context.

Plan.description: The unit's plan — a workspace path to a plan doc and/or an inline prompt. Chat carries a prompt; routines carry a plan ref.

Output.description: The unit's output bus binding — the ws.v1 per-unit topic the unit publishes on, and which modes it uses.

topic.description: the ws.v1 per-unit topic, e.g. unit:<id>:out

Invocation.description: One agent-runtime-unit invocation. The dispatcher emits it (from any trigger source) and agent-api turns it into a runtime.v1 agent worker.

subject.description: identity.v1 subject = the PERSON = quota owner (VEXA_OWNER) + cred-brokerage key

workspace_repo.description: the per-person workspace.v1 git repo URL the unit commits to

workspace_ref.description: the branch the worker checks out

tools.description: the scoped toolbelt (tool.v1 names) granted to this invocation — the --allowedTools allow-set
